\caption{Falsification controls for the ULM localization-derived structural audit. The first block reports the intended vessel-core versus perivascular-shell masks. The second applies a large cyclic translation to both masks without changing their size. The third redraws vessel and background labels at random inside the same support mask while preserving the per-block class counts. All rows use the same frozen 64-frame no-registration whitened profile and the same MC--SVD residual windows; only the structural masks change. The translated-mask block is a misregistration control rather than a pure chance control, because the shifted masks can still land on other vessel-rich anatomy within the same acquisition. The support-matched permutation is therefore the cleaner chance-like falsification for testing whether the reported structural signal depends on vascular alignment rather than generic score morphology.}
